_note: AU patient-record provider directory — a FIRST-PARTY named build (FLOW_PLAN H1 'AU provider metadata must be built'). This is structured metadata only: SMART App Launch endpoints + a REFERENCE (client_id_ref) to a secrets-manager key, NEVER a client secret, token, or credential (<security_and_secrets>). Every provider ships status 'input_gated' until an operator supplies conformance evidence + credentials; live connection is not opened by this file. Endpoints use the sanctioned example.invalid placeholder until real values are injected at deploy time.

providers.name: My Health Record (Australian Digital Health Agency)
providers.status_reason: Live MHR connection requires ADHA conformance registration, a registered OAuth2 client, and credentials via the secrets manager — none of which this repo holds. Metadata scaffold only.
providers.default_scope: launch/patient patient/Observation.read patient/Condition.read patient/MedicationRequest.read offline_access openid fhirUser
providers.conformance: AU Core 0.3.0 (structural validation via fhir-broker fhir_validate; ValueSet binding pending live NCTS)
providers.notes: My Health Record is the primary AU longitudinal source. Real endpoints + client registration are injected at deploy time; the id/scope/profile shape here is what the SMART App Launch flow will use.

providers.name: Public HAPI FHIR R4 sandbox (synthetic data)
providers.status_reason: Public, open, synthetic-data-only FHIR R4 server — the H1 smoke target. NEVER production (see fhir-broker/live-backend.js PUBLIC_SANDBOX_HOSTS refusal).
providers.conformance: Generic R4 (not AU Core profiled) — smoke/plumbing verification only, not clinical grounding
providers.notes: Open synthetic sandbox for proving the wso2 live-backend plumbing end to end without credentials. Data here is synthetic and unprofiled; it validates transport, not AU conformance.
